entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: Pennisetum glaucum [L.] R. Br., the crop subjected to recurrent selection for yield and disease resistance

entities.name: RECURRENT SELECTION
entities.description: A breeding method involving multiple cycles of selection to improve specific traits

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced per unit area, which was increased through selection

entities.name: RESISTANCE TO DOWNY MILDEW
entities.description: The ability of the plant to resist infection by the pathogen Sclerospora graminicola

entities.name: SCLEROSPORA GRAMINICOLA
entities.description: The fungal pathogen that causes downy mildew in pearl millet

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the multilocational yield trials and disease nurseries were conducted

entities.name: TERMINAL DROUGHT
entities.description: A condition of moisture stress occurring at the end of the crop growth cycle

entities.name: OPTIMAL MOISTURE
entities.description: Ideal water availability conditions for crop growth

entities.name: DOWNY MILDEW INFECTION
entities.description: The state of being infected by the downy mildew pathogen, used to test resistance

entities.name: BIOMASS
entities.description: The total biological mass of the plant, associated with grain yield increases

entities.name: HARVEST INDEX
entities.description: The ratio of grain yield to total biomass, associated with grain yield increases

entities.name: GROWTH DURATION
entities.description: The length of time from planting to maturity

entities.name: YIELD STABILITY
entities.description: The consistency of grain yield across different environments and locations

entities.name: MULTILOCATIONAL YIELD TRIALS
entities.description: The process of testing crop performance across multiple geographic locations

relationships.description: is the pathogen that the trait resists
